_README: Global settings for the checks and guards. Copy to `checks-config.json` at the repo root and adjust. Every key is OPTIONAL — set only what you change; an absent file or key means the defaults below (built into the scripts, today's behavior). A present-but-broken file is a BLOCKING finding (CFG-INVALID), never silently ignored. Method-calibration constants (index-eval scorer/sufficiency thresholds) are deliberately NOT here: they define what the metric means and are not per-project taste.
_audit: When the deterministic report (`checks/decisions-audit.py --report`) recommends spending a tier-2 (LLM) audit, and how it batches. `volume-alert` = entry count per channel at which the channel is worth auditing; `pending-*` = ratification-inbox nudges; `pending-stale-days` also drives the stale marker of `memory-audit.py --pending`.
_sizes: Granularity signals (TO-CONFIRM, never blocking): an oversized entry is a hint that its content belongs in the durable doc, not a fault. Units: lines of useful content, words for labels.
_guards: EXTENSION-ONLY, fail-closed: a repo-local config can ADD surveillance (extra instruction files for the poisoning scan, extra path-regex allowlist entries for the secret scan) but can never disable a guard or remove a built-in pattern — otherwise poisoned content could switch off the alarm by editing this file. An invalid entry (bad regex) is skipped, the guard keeps running.
_doc-refs: Tuning for `checks/doc-refs-check.py`. Every key OPTIONAL and ADDITIVE — absent ⇒ today's behavior. `ignore-prefixes` (R-DEAD-PATH) = prefixes of tokens that LOOK like repo paths but never are — a runtime API joined to a filename (`Runtime.dataDir/save.txt`) or a machine-local path in a guide. The next three tune the two SYMBOL rules (R-DEAD-SYMBOL / R-GHOST-ABSENCE), whose corpus is a host ecosystem's own — Unity ≠ React ≠ Django — so, like index-config's `roots`/`extensions`, the framework never hardcodes them. `symbol-suffixes` = when NON-EMPTY, a `PascalCase` candidate is kept only if it ends with one of these (a project's type-naming convention: `Manager`, `View`, `Registry`…); empty ⇒ every composed PascalCase token, as before — this is the lever that silences a host API cited across the docs. `ignore-symbols` = literal candidate exclusions for host-ecosystem API (`MonoBehaviour`, `RectTransform`…), additive not substitutive. `symbol-ignore-dirs` = doc dirs (relative to the framework root) where the two symbol rules are muted — transient docs that legitimately name not-yet-built types (`backlog/`); R-DEAD-PATH / R-DEAD-DECISION stay ACTIVE there.
_memory-graph: Settings for `hooks/memory-graph.py`. `channels-base` = the subdir the four memory channels live under, for a project that nests them (e.g. "Docs" → Docs/decisions/…); "" (default) = repo root, the framework's own layout. `self-extra-dirs` = tooling dirs to also self-suppress nudges on (default ["checks","hooks","adapters"]; a Claude Code project might use [".claude"]). `class-file-extensions` = extensions whose basename equals a source symbol (one-symbol-per-file), e.g. [".java"]; empty (default) → covers matches by cited-path containment only, fully language-agnostic. `code-roots` = dirs the class/tag ambiguity guard scans (default: index-config roots, else the repo).
_closure: Definition of Done, step 3 (`backlog/README.md`). The framework ALWAYS runs its own tier-1 checks at closure; `review` only answers for the PROJECT's review of the delivered surface. Tri-state: key absent or empty = the agent ASKS at each closure (the default — the standard requires the review but never assumes the answer, `INSTALL.md §Guiding principle`); a non-empty string = a standing yes, printed in place of the generic wording (say what reviewing means here: which skill, which agent, which scope); `false` = a standing waiver, printed as such so the choice stays visible. Any other value falls back to asking. There is deliberately NO mechanical check that the review happened — unverifiable without false positives, so out of tier 1 by construction. Example of a declared value: 'standards-auditor agent over the whole work-item surface, not just the last diff'.
